src_7ed57d1c-d0a7-4218-b05a-16332ad2730b.title: Apuntes: Objetos Directos y Los Pronombres de los Objetos Directos
src_7ed57d1c-d0a7-4218-b05a-16332ad2730b.content:
Vocabulario importante:
Objeto Directo: Direct Object 
Pronombre del Objeto Directo: Direct Object Pronoun
La “A”  personal: The Personal “A” 

¿Qué es un objeto directo?

Objeto Directo: Is a noun that recives the action of the verb. 
The objeto directo is generally found after the verb.
Ejemplo: 
Luisa y Valentina toman fotos. / Luisa y Valentina are taking pictures.
Objeto Directo: fotos

What if we were unsure what the objeto directo is?  What could we do? 
To find the objeto directo, we can ask ourselves: ¿Qué toman Luisa y Valentia or What are Luisa and Valentina taking?
The answer to the question is the objeto directo: Fotos/Pictures
Individual Practice to find objeto directo:
Nosotros comemos la pizza / We eat the pizza
Pregunta: ¿Qué Comemos? / What are we eating
Objeto Directo: La pizza/ the pizza 
Los objetos directos y la “a” Personal

When the objeto directo is a person or a pet , it is proceeded by an “ a ”. This “a” is called the personal “a”. 
There is no English equivalent for the “a” personal.
Ejemplos: 
Yo miro a Marco / I am looking at Marco.
Yo miro la televisión / I am watching TV. 
Ana ama a su abuela / Ana loves her grandmother
Ana ama la clase de español / Ana loves Spanish class[
Pronombres de los Objetos Directos
Why do we use pronombres de los objetos directos? 
We use pronombres de los objetos directos to replace a objeto directo. 
We do this so that we avoid repeating a noun already mentioned.
Ejemplos en inglés: 
I have the book. ➡️ I have it. 
My mom drives my friends and me to school. ➡️ My mom drives us  to school. 
Carla brings plates, forks, and napkins to the picnic. ➡️Carla brings them  to the picnic. 
Los Pronombres de los Objetos Directos en Español

Where does los pronombres de los objetos directos appear in a affirmative sentences? 
Generally los pronombres de los objetos directos appear in front of the conjugated verb
Ejemplo: 
Vicky tiene la llave
Objeto Directo: la llave
El pronombre del objeto directo: La
Nueva frase: Vicky la tiene
Where does los pronombre de los objetos directos appear in a negative sentences?
Generally, los pronombres de los objetos directos appear after the “no” and in fronthh of the conjugated verb.
Ejemplo: 
Ellos no escuchan a Pablo y a mí
Objeto Directo: Pablo y mi
El pronombre del objeto directo: Los
Nueva frase: Ellos no los eschuchan

q_05e11955-25bb-44eb-8738-e896dadcc141.prompt: What are the three primary reasons cells divide?
q_05e11955-25bb-44eb-8738-e896dadcc141.answer: Growth of an organism, repair and replacement of tissues, and reproduction (asexual or gamete production).

q_ea6661c4-7662-4ccc-a214-90ab896537a6.prompt: What is the significance of the G1 checkpoint?
q_ea6661c4-7662-4ccc-a214-90ab896537a6.answer: It assesses environmental conditions and DNA integrity; if favorable, the cell proceeds to S phase; otherwise, it can pause or enter G0.

q_badc2b6b-33a5-458b-aee8-a2207b1e4288.prompt: Describe the main events that occur during S phase of the cell cycle.
q_badc2b6b-33a5-458b-aee8-a2207b1e4288.answer: DNA replication occurs, resulting in each chromosome being duplicated into two sister chromatids.

q_49901dce-43b0-42ea-bc8b-4e6b691fa94b.prompt: What is the role of the G2 checkpoint?
q_49901dce-43b0-42ea-bc8b-4e6b691fa94b.answer: It verifies that DNA replication is complete and that DNA is not damaged before mitosis begins.

q_291e9bbd-9973-40bd-8f6d-9a94f3768993.prompt: Outline the key stages of mitosis, in order.

q_3afd196f-c3cd-42dd-b887-6d9e8df96e6e.prompt: What is cytokinesis and how does it differ in animal vs. plant cells?
q_3afd196f-c3cd-42dd-b887-6d9e8df96e6e.answer: Cytokinesis divides the cytoplasm to form two daughter cells; animal cells use a contractile ring, while plant cells use a cell plate.

q_ca2e7302-78b1-4886-9cfe-519cc7206042.prompt: What are cyclins and CDKs, and how do they regulate the cell cycle?
q_ca2e7302-78b1-4886-9cfe-519cc7206042.answer: Cyclins are regulatory proteins that activate CDKs, forming complexes that drive phase transitions.

q_d583572e-2ca0-412c-a069-ae42c3616886.prompt: Explain the role of p53 in maintaining genomic stability.
q_d583572e-2ca0-412c-a069-ae42c3616886.answer: p53 monitors DNA damage; if detected, it can induce cell cycle arrest, DNA repair, or apoptosis.

q_faeeb55f-1610-4aff-a43d-a68087890956.prompt: Describe the major types of DNA repair mechanisms.
q_faeeb55f-1610-4aff-a43d-a68087890956.answer: Base excision repair, nucleotide excision repair, and mismatch repair; double-strand break repair via homologous recombination and non-homologous end joining.

q_7ac9d119-b019-415c-9737-f69d8c885a8d.prompt: How does meiosis differ from mitosis in terms of chromosome number and genetic diversity?
q_7ac9d119-b019-415c-9737-f69d8c885a8d.answer: Meiosis produces 4 genetically distinct haploid cells, while mitosis produces 2 genetically identical diploid cells.

q_31e1a708-5afb-4fda-a88f-8c5c566bc393.prompt: What is the central dogma of molecular biology?

q_14a3569b-1ca3-4508-9425-98d551ecd26f.prompt: Name at least three epigenetic modifications and their general effects on gene expression.
q_14a3569b-1ca3-4508-9425-98d551ecd26f.answer: DNA methylation (often represses), histone modifications (acetylation generally activates), and chromatin remodeling.

q_4fa33fbf-f400-4007-907b-74115441335e.prompt: Describe the purpose of protein quality control mechanisms.
q_4fa33fbf-f400-4007-907b-74115441335e.answer: Misfolded proteins are refolded or degraded through mechanisms like ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation; autophagy removes damaged organelles.

q_9c145685-0331-4264-a028-1fdeede17b7c.prompt: Summarize the three stages of cellular respiration and their net ATP production.
q_9c145685-0331-4264-a028-1fdeede17b7c.answer: Glycolysis (2 ATP), Citric Acid Cycle (2 ATP), Oxidative Phosphorylation (around 32-34 ATP – most of ATP produced)

src_4a6c32b3-88cb-41ff-9310-a6553c64f289.content: Photosynthesis is how plants convert sunlight into chemical energy. Chlorophyll absorbs light, and the Calvin cycle helps produce glucose. Mitochondria generate ATP for cell processes.

src_af2ddfc0-57c1-428e-95e5-a75c6262369c.title: Ollama live check
src_af2ddfc0-57c1-428e-95e5-a75c6262369c.content: Mitosis has phases: prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase. Cytokinesis divides the cytoplasm. DNA replication occurs before mitosis during interphase. Chromosomes align at the metaphase plate and sister chromatids separate in anaphase.
